auditor: Chartered Professional Accountants, Licensed Public Accountants
economic_notes: Economic development segment includes motel, business centre, and fuel sales. COVID-19 caused lockdowns, travel restrictions, and delays in capital projects. Deferred revenue increased significantly due to pandemic-related funding flexibility.
notes: Audit is qualified due to absence of budget information as required by PSAS Section 1201 — same qualification applied in prior year. Auditor firm name not explicitly stated beyond 'Chartered Professional Accountants, Licensed Public Accountants' based in Thunder Bay, Ontario. No schedule of remuneration included. Housing and infrastructure expenses combined into one segment. Large deferred revenue balance of $10.96M primarily from ISC. Significant capital construction in progress of $21.04M. COVID-19 note highlights ongoing dependency on ISC and other funders to service debt. Net debt position of $480,538. Prior year ISC revenue recognized was $14,623,523 per Note 15 but statement shows $17,750,478 federal transfers — difference reflects other federal funders like Public Safety Canada and CMHC included in statement total.
